steps.title: Translate the notation
steps.content: $f(6)$ means the $y$-value of the graph when $x=6$. So the task is not to solve an equation; it is to read the height of the graph above $x=6$.

steps.title: Read the graph
steps.content: On the graph, move to $x=6$ on the horizontal axis. The curve reaches its lowest point there, at the grid point $(6,2)$. Therefore $$f(6)=2.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The answer choice that gives $2$ is choice B.
